hypothesis: If a reinforcement-learning generative model maximizes CATS-based pharmacophore similarity to known estrogen receptor antagonists while penalizing MACCS/MAP4-based structural similarity and optimizing QED, then it will generate novel compounds with superior ER (PDB 8AWG) docking scores, favorable QED and SA, and lower database overlap than a QED+QVina docking baseline because pharmacophore guidance preserves activity-relevant features while structural dissimilarity enforces novelty.
